envelope.question: 想做个什么样的 ultraapp？先选个大方向，后面我再细问。

options.label: 媒体处理（视频剪辑、音频转换、图片批处理）
AI 生成（文生图、文生视频、文本改写）
数据处理（CSV/JSON 转换、爬虫、报表）
文档工作流（PDF 抽取、翻译、总结）

envelope.context: 这是第一题，先确定大类，方便后面问输入格式、依赖工具（ffmpeg / LLM / pandoc 等）。如果你已经有现成的样例文件或脚本，可以直接上传/贴路径，我会用 extract_metadata 看一眼。

freeform: Build me a research-summariser pipeline: paste a long article, the LLM extracts named entities (people/orgs/places), categorises each, and renders a markdown report with sections per category. Multi-step LLM pipeline; uses Anthropic.

value.name: research-summariser
value.title: Research Summariser
value.description: Paste a long article; an Anthropic LLM extracts named entities (people, orgs, places), categorises them, and renders a markdown report with sections per category.

value.name: report
value.description: Markdown report grouped by entity category (people / orgs / places).

steps.description: Anthropic LLM extracts raw named entities from the article (surface form + sentence context).
Anthropic LLM categorises each entity as person / org / place and dedupes.
Render a markdown file with one section per category; under each, list entities with a 1-line context snippet.

envelope.question: Besides pasting the article text, do you also want to accept file uploads or URLs?

options.label: Paste only — keep it simple
Paste + .txt/.md file upload
Paste + file upload + URL fetch
Paste + .pdf upload (extract text first)

envelope.context: You said 'paste a long article', so paste is in. Adding .txt/.md upload is ~free (no extra deps). PDF needs pdf-parse / pdfminer; URL fetch needs an HTTP client + readability extractor — both are real adds, so I'd only pull them in if you actually need them.

value.name: articleText
value.description: Paste the article text directly. Either this or articleFile is required.

value.name: articleFile
value.description: Upload a .txt or .md file. Either this or articleText is required.

args.value: Coalesce: if articleFile is provided, read its UTF-8 contents; otherwise use articleText.

envelope.question: Which entity categories should the report break out?

options.label: People / Organisations / Places (just the three you mentioned)
+ Dates & Events
+ Dates, Events, Products/Technologies, Works (books/papers/films)
Let the LLM decide categories per article (open-ended)

envelope.context: You named people/orgs/places. Sticking to a fixed set makes the categorise step deterministic and the markdown layout predictable. Open-ended categories give richer reports but the section list will vary article to article — harder to scan if you process many.

envelope.question: For each entity in the report, what detail do you want shown?

options.label: Just the name
Name + 1-line context (the sentence it first appears in)
Name + LLM-written 1-2 sentence description of its role in the article
Name + mention count + LLM description

envelope.context: This shapes both prompts and the report's information density. 'Name + context' is cheap (one extraction call total). 'Name + description' is a true summariser — the LLM has to reason about each entity's role, costing one extra LLM call per entity (or one batched call). Given you called this a 'research-summariser', recommending the description variant.

args.value: Anthropic LLM categorises each entity (person/organisation/place), dedupes, and writes a 1-2 sentence description of each entity's role in the article. Single batched call given the full article + raw entity list.
Render a markdown file with three sections (People / Organisations / Places). Under each, list entities with their LLM-written description.

envelope.question: Which Anthropic model should the pipeline use?

options.label: Claude Sonnet (4.7) — balanced cost & quality
Claude Haiku — cheapest, fastest, good enough for extraction
Claude Opus — best quality, ~5x cost
Mixed — Haiku for extraction, Sonnet for description

envelope.context: Two LLM calls per article (extract + categorise/describe). On a 5K-word article, Sonnet runs ~$0.02/call and finishes in <10s. Haiku is ~5x cheaper but more likely to miss entities or hallucinate descriptions. Mixed is a real win if you're processing volume.

envelope.question: How should the UI be laid out?

options.label: Single form — paste/upload + 'Generate' button, report appears below
Split view — input on the left, live report preview on the right
Wizard — step 1 input, step 2 entity review/edit, step 3 report

envelope.context: Pipeline is fully automated (no human-in-the-loop steps), so a single form fits cleanly. Split-view is nicer if you'll iterate on the same article multiple times. Wizard only makes sense if you want to manually approve/edit the entity list before description — which you didn't ask for.

summary:
Spec is complete. Quick recap:

- **Research Summariser** — paste article text or upload .txt/.md; produces a markdown report.
- **Pipeline (3 steps, Sonnet 4.7 ×2 calls):** extract entities → categorise + describe (person/org/place) → render markdown with three sections.
- **UI:** single-form, progress bar; no extra binary deps; ~20s per article.
